description: Canonical terms, prohibited synonyms, and product vocabulary for all content outputs.

usage.scope: All skills reference this file for terminology consistency.
rules: Use canonical term in all outputs.
Context determines which term set applies.

select.context: Choosing from a list or dropdown

remove.context: Non-destructive removal from view or list

tailored-health-cash-plan.context: Use title case when referring to this specific product. Use lowercase 'health cash plan' only when referring to cash plans in general, not this product specifically.

select-health-cash-plan.context: Use title case when referring to this specific product. Do not shorten to 'Select' alone in member-facing or marketing content.

flexible-health-cash-plan.context: Use title case when referring to this specific product. Use lowercase 'health cash plan' only in general references.

corporate-health-cash-plan.context: Use title case when referring to this specific product. Do not abbreviate or shorten in any member-facing, employer-facing, or marketing content.

gp-anytime.context: Additional benefit included with health cash plans. Provides 24/7 access to GP appointments via phone or video. Provided by Health Hero. Always write as 'GP Anytime' with both words capitalised.

employee-assistance-programme.context: Additional benefit providing confidential guidance and expert support. Acceptable to abbreviate as 'EAP' after first use. Provided by Health Assured or Spectrum.Life depending on plan. Do not use US spelling 'Program'.

247-counselling-and-support-helpline.context: Additional benefit providing round-the-clock confidential counselling. Provided by Health Assured or Spectrum.Life depending on plan. Always write in full on first reference. Use British spelling 'Counselling', not 'Counseling'.

skinvision.context: Additional benefit. AI-enabled service for skin cancer risk checking and tailored health advice. Provided by SkinVision B.V. Always written as one word with capital S and capital V.

perks.context: Additional benefit providing exclusive shopping savings and discounts on top brands. Provided by BenefitHub. Always written in full capitals.

home-assistance.context: Additional benefit providing hands-on support after a hospital stay of two or more nights. Provided by Grace Consulting. Always written with both words capitalised.

mygym-discounts.context: Additional benefit offering discounts on gym memberships and digital fitness subscriptions across 4,300 UK locations. Provided by Epassi. Always written as one word with capital M, G, and D.

on-demand-physio.context: Additional benefit providing physiotherapy consultations via phone or video. Provided by PhysioMed. Always hyphenate 'On-Demand'. Both words capitalised.

mental-wellbeing-support.context: Additional benefit. The Wisdom app provides personalised wellness tracking, mental health improvement, and resilience tools. Provided by Health Assured. Refer to the benefit as 'Mental Wellbeing Support' in content. The app name 'Wisdom' may be used in instructional context when directing members to access it.

digital-wellbeing-platform.context: Additional benefit providing self-help resources, fitness tracking, meditation, and live fitness classes. Provided by Spectrum.Life. Always written with all three words capitalised.

thrive-mental-wellbeing.context: Always use the full name 'Thrive Mental Wellbeing'. Never shorten to 'Thrive' alone. When describing the product, use the approved description: an NHS digitally compliant app for the screening and self-management of mental health conditions. The NHS digitally compliant claim must always be accompanied by the footnote: 'Complies with NHS Digital Technology Assessment Criteria'. Provided by Thrive Therapeutic Software Ltd.

thrive-mental-wellbeing-support.context: Additional benefit variant. Provides access to evidence-based techniques and tools to help manage mental health. Provided by Thrive Therapeutic Software Ltd. Use this name when referring to the standard tier of Thrive access without coaching.

thrive-mental-wellbeing-support-with-coa.canonical: Thrive Mental Wellbeing Support with Coaching
thrive-mental-wellbeing-support-with-coa.context: Additional benefit variant. Provides evidence-based techniques plus access to a human-led therapy service, including in-app chat with a consultant psychiatrist or psychologist. Provided by Thrive Therapeutic Software Ltd. Always use the full name to distinguish from the standard tier.

cancer-screening.context: Additional benefit providing early cancer detection through a self-administered finger-prick blood test kit. Provided by Prevent Limited, trading as Health Shield. Always written with both words capitalised.

mywellness.context: Legacy name for the suite of additional benefits previously branded under this name. Health Shield is in the process of phasing out this term. Do not lead new content with the MyWellness name. Where existing references remain, note that services may differ by plan type and can change without notice. Check with the Content and Brand team before using this name in new content.

caring.definition: We put people at the heart of everything we do, writing with genuine compassion and a desire to help everyone feel secure, supported, and valued.

warm.definition: We write in a friendly, approachable way that feels personal and human, like a trusted friend who knows their subject and genuinely wants to help.

friendly.definition: We use everyday language that is easy to read and comfortable to engage with, keeping things conversational and relatable without tipping into slang or over-informality.
friendly.contrast: Friendly, not formal. 'Make sure', not 'ensure'. 'Speedy', not 'prompt'.

professional.definition: We are credible, clear, and respectful in everything we write, grounding our content in honest information and measured claims rather than jargon, hype, or vague reassurance.
professional.contrast: Professional, not distant. 'People', not 'resources'. 'We've paid too much', not 'overpayment'.
